    29→    CodeBlockNotFoundError,
    30→    EmbeddingLimitReachedError,
    31→    ExternalServiceError,
    32→    NamespaceResolutionError,
    33→    TaskNotFoundError,
    34→)
    35→from app.lib.bulk_grader import run_bulk_grader
    36→from app.lib.claude_client import (
    37→    analyze_code,
    38→    generate_agent_review,
    39→    generate_discoverable_name,
    40→    generate_query_guesses,
    41→)
    42→from app.lib.database import (
    43→    check_embedding_limit_async,
    44→    delete_snippet_pg,
    45→    find_duplicate_snippet_async,
    46→    increment_user_embedding_count_async,
    47→    record_snippet_execution_async,
    48→    register_snippet_source_hash,
    49→    upsert_snippet,
    50→)
    51→from app.lib.embeddings import (
    52→    create_all_facet_texts,
    53→    embed,
    54→    embed_batch,
    55→)
    56→from app.lib.hash_utils import generate_id, hash_dict, hash_obj, hash_str, truncate_for_storage, truncate_logs_for_ai
    57→from app.lib.metrics import record_code_block_stored, record_verdict
    58→from app.lib.namespace_resolver import resolve_retrieval_namespaces
    59→from app.lib.pinecone_client import (
    60→    delete_code_block_with_facets,
    61→    fetch_code_block,
    62→    fetch_task_pattern,
    63→    upsert_code_block_multi_vector,
    64→    upsert_execution,
    65→    upsert_golden_example,
    66→    upsert_task_pattern,
    67→)
    68→from app.lib.redaction import redact_sensitive_data, summarize_redactions
    69→from app.lib.sparse_encoder import create_code_search_text, encode_sparse
    70→from app.lib.task_status import TaskStatusResponse, get_status, mark_completed, mark_failed, register_task
    71→from app.lib.verbatim_cache import invalidate_verbatim_cache_async
    72→from app.middleware.quota import _get_tier, enforce_public_quota, enforce_quota
    73→from app.middleware.rate_limit import STORE_RATE_LIMIT, limiter
    74→from app.models.code_blocks import (
    75→    AgentVerdict,
    76→    AutoReviewRequest,
    77→    AutoReviewResponse,
    78→    BulkExecutionResultRequest,
    79→    BulkExecutionResultResponse,
    80→    CodeBlock,
    81→    DeleteCodeBlockResponse,
    82→    ExecutionState,
    83→    FileWritten,
    84→    LogFile,
    85→    RecordCacheUsageRequest,
    86→    RecordCacheUsageResponse,
    87→    StoreCodeBlockRequest,
    88→    StoreCodeBlockResponse,
    89→    StoreExecutionRequest,
    90→    StoreExecutionResponse,
    91→    SubmitExecutionResultRequest,
    92→    SubmitExecutionResultResponse,
    93→    SyntheticQueriesRequest,
    94→    SyntheticQueriesResponse,
    95→)
    96→from app.models.organizations import OrganizationTier, SnipsDesired
    97→from app.tasks.synthetic_queries import populate_namespace, process_code_block_synthetics
    98→from app.tasks.voting import _vote_on_execution_async, apply_vote_verdict, vote_on_execution
    99→
   100→router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/store", tags=["storage"])
   101→
   102→
   103→# ============================================================================
   104→# Background Task Status
   105→# ============================================================================
   106→
   107→
   108→@router.get("/status/{task_id}", response_model=TaskStatusResponse)
   109→async def get_task_status(task_id: str) -> TaskStatusResponse:
   110→    """Check the status of a background storage task.
   111→
   112→    Returns the current status (pending/completed/failed) and error details
   113→    if the task failed. Status entries expire after 1 hour.
   114→    """
   115→    status = get_status(task_id)
   116→    if not status:
   117→        raise TaskNotFoundError(task_id)
   118→    return status
   119→
   120→
   121→def get_storage_namespace(user_info: dict, base_type: str, public: bool = False) -> str:
   122→    """
   123→    Get the namespace to store to based on user info.
   124→
   125→    Args:
   126→        user_info: User info from auth middleware (includes tier, org, workspace)
   127→        base_type: Type of namespace (code_blocks, task_patterns, etc.)
   128→        public: Route to public_snips namespace (community-shared snippets)
   129→
   130→    Returns:
   131→        Single namespace string to store to
   132→
   133→    Raises:
   134→        RaysurferError: If namespace cannot be resolved
   135→    """
   136→    # Public uploads go directly to public_snips namespace
   137→    if public and base_type == "code_blocks":
   138→        from app.lib.namespace_resolver import PUBLIC_SNIPS_NAMESPACE
   139→
   140→        return PUBLIC_SNIPS_NAMESPACE
   141→
   142→    # Check for custom namespace override first (X-Raysurfer-Namespace header)
   143→    override_namespace = user_info.get("override_namespace")
   144→    if override_namespace:
   145→        return override_namespace
   146→
   147→    # Get tier from user info (organization_tier from validate_api_key, or tier for dev mode)
   148→    tier_str = user_info.get("organization_tier") or user_info.get("tier", "free")
   149→    if tier_str == "dev" or tier_str is None:
   150→        tier = OrganizationTier.FREE
   151→    else:
   152→        tier = OrganizationTier(tier_str)
   153→
   154→    # Get org/workspace IDs (use overrides if present)
   155→    org_id = user_info.get("override_organization_id") or user_info.get("organization_id")
   156→    workspace_id = user_info.get("override_workspace_id") or user_info.get("client_workspace_id")
   157→
   158→    # Extract user_id for per-user namespace resolution
   159→    user_id = user_info.get("user_id")
   160→
   161→    # Get snips_desired from headers (determines where to store)
   162→    snips_desired_str = user_info.get("snips_desired")
   163→    snips_desired = SnipsDesired(snips_desired_str) if snips_desired_str else None
   164→
   165→    # Resolve namespaces using the same logic as retrieval
   166→    retrieval_ns = resolve_retrieval_namespaces(
   167→        tier=tier,
   168→        organization_id=UUID(org_id) if org_id else None,
   169→        workspace_id=workspace_id,
   170→        public_snips=False,  # Storage always goes to private namespace
   171→        snips_desired=snips_desired,
   172→        user_id=UUID(user_id) if user_id else None,
   173→    )
   174→    namespaces = getattr(retrieval_ns, base_type)
   175→    if not namespaces:
   176→        raise NamespaceResolutionError(base_type)
   177→    # For workspace isolation, retrieval returns [org, workspace] - storage goes to workspace (last)
   178→    # Check workspace_id presence since backend auto-infers CLIENT mode
   179→    if workspace_id and len(namespaces) > 1:
   180→        return namespaces[-1]  # Workspace namespace (isolated)
   181→    return namespaces[0]
   182→
   183→
   184→# ============================================================================
   185→# Store Code Block
   186→# ============================================================================
   187→
   188→
   189→@router.post("/code-block", response_model=StoreCodeBlockResponse)
   190→@limiter.limit(STORE_RATE_LIMIT)
   191→async def store_code_block(
   192→    request: Request,
   193→    body: StoreCodeBlockRequest,
   194→    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
   195→    user_info: dict = Depends(enforce_quota),
   196→) -> StoreCodeBlockResponse:
   197→    """Store a new code block - returns immediately, processing happens in background"""
   198→    # Generate ID immediately so we can return it
   199→    block_id = generate_id("cb")
   200→
   201→    # Extract user_id for metadata tracking
   202→    user_id = user_info.get("user_id")
   203→
   204→    # Check embedding limit for free tier users
   205→    tier_str = user_info.get("organization_tier") or user_info.get("tier", "free")
   206→    if user_id and tier_str in ("free", "dev", None):
   207→        allowed, current, limit = await check_embedding_limit_async(UUID(user_id), tier_str or "free")
   208→        if not allowed:
   209→            raise EmbeddingLimitReachedError(current=current, limit=limit)
   210→
   211→    # Get the namespace to store to based on user's org/tier
   212→    namespace = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "code_blocks", public=body.public)
   213→
   214→    # Enforce public upload quota/billing
   215→    if body.public:
   216→        tier = _get_tier(user_info)
   217→        if tier == "free" and user_id:
   218→            await enforce_public_quota(user_id, "public_upload", tier)
   219→        if tier == "pro":
   220→            from app.lib.credit_service import get_credit_balance
   221→
   222→            org_id = user_info.get("organization_id")
   223→            if org_id:
   224→                balance = await get_credit_balance(UUID(org_id))
   225→                if balance < 5:
   226→                    from app.errors import InsufficientCreditError
   227→
   228→                    raise InsufficientCreditError(balance_cents=balance, required_cents=5, org_id=org_id)
   229→            request.state.credit_operation = "public_upload"
   230→
   231→    # Extract organization_id for metadata tracking
   232→    organization_id = user_info.get("organization_id")
   233→
   234→    # Queue all heavy processing for background execution
   235→    background_tasks.add_task(
   236→        _process_code_block_storage,
   237→        block_id=block_id,
   238→        body=body,
   239→        user_id=user_id,
   240→        organization_id=organization_id,
   241→        namespace=namespace,
   242→    )
   243→
   244→    return StoreCodeBlockResponse(
   245→        success=True,
   246→        code_block_id=block_id,
   247→        embedding_id=block_id,
   248→        message="Code block storage queued",
   249→        status_url=f"/api/store/status/{block_id}",
   250→    )
   251→
   252→
   253→MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH = 30
   254→
   255→_TRIVIAL_BODY_TOKENS = frozenset({"pass", "...", "return", "return None"})
   256→_TRIVIAL_MIN_MEANINGFUL_LINES = 1
   257→
   258→
   259→def is_trivial_code(source: str) -> bool:
   260→    """Reject code where every function/method body is a stub (pass, ..., empty return)."""
   261→    if not source or not source.strip():
   262→        return True
   263→    lines = source.strip().splitlines()
   264→    # Strip away comments, blank lines, imports, and string-only lines
   265→    meaningful = []
   266→    for raw in lines:
   267→        s = raw.strip()
   268→        if not s or s.startswith("#") or s.startswith(("import ", "from ")):
   269→            continue
   270→        if s in ('"""', "'''"):
   271→            continue
   272→        meaningful.append(s)
   273→    # Collect lines that are NOT def/class signatures and NOT trivial body tokens
   274→    body_lines = [
   275→        line
   276→        for line in meaningful
   277→        if not re.match(r"^(async\s+)?def\s+|^class\s+", line) and line not in _TRIVIAL_BODY_TOKENS
   278→    ]
   279→    return len(body_lines) < _TRIVIAL_MIN_MEANINGFUL_LINES
   280→
   281→
   282→async def _process_code_block_storage(
   283→    block_id: str,
   284→    body: StoreCodeBlockRequest,
   285→    user_id: str | None = None,
   286→    organization_id: str | None = None,
   287→    namespace: str | None = None,
   288→) -> None:
   289→    """Background task to process and store a code block"""
   290→    register_task(block_id, "code_block_storage")
   291→    try:
   292→        # Reject snippets with source too short to be useful
   293→        if body.source and len(body.source) < MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH:
   294→            print(
   295→                f"[{block_id}] Rejected short source ({len(body.source)} chars < {MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH}), skipping storage"
   296→            )
   297→            mark_completed(block_id)
   298→            return
   299→
   300→        # Reject trivial/stub code (e.g. "def helper(): pass")
   301→        if body.source and is_trivial_code(body.source):
   302→            print(f"[{block_id}] Rejected trivial code, skipping storage")
   303→            mark_completed(block_id)
   304→            return
   305→
   306→        # Apply auto-redaction if requested (opt-in)
   307→        source_to_store = body.source
   308→        redaction_summary: dict[str, int] = {}
   309→        if body.auto_redact and body.source:
   310→            redacted_source, redactions = redact_sensitive_data(body.source)
   311→            if redactions:
   312→                redaction_summary = summarize_redactions(redactions)
   313→                source_to_store = redacted_source
   314→                print(f"[{block_id}] Auto-redacted {len(redactions)} sensitive items: {redaction_summary}")
   315→
   316→        # Skip if the same source already exists in this namespace
   317→        if source_to_store and namespace:
   318→            try:
   319→                existing_id = await find_duplicate_snippet_async(source_to_store, namespace)
   320→                if existing_id:
   321→                    print(f"[{block_id}] Duplicate detected (existing={existing_id}), skipping AI processing")
   322→                    mark_completed(block_id)
   323→                    return
   324→            except Exception as e:
   325→                print(f"[{block_id}] Dedup check failed (non-fatal), proceeding: {e}")
   326→
   327→        now = datetime.utcnow()
   328→
   329→        # Optionally analyze code to infer capabilities
   330→        inferred_capabilities = body.capabilities or []
   331→        inferred_tags = body.tags or []
   332→        inferred_description = body.description
   333→
   334→        if source_to_store and (not body.capabilities or len(body.capabilities) == 0):
   335→            try:
   336→                analysis = await analyze_code(source_to_store, body.entrypoint)
   337→                inferred_capabilities = analysis.inferred_capabilities
   338→                inferred_tags = analysis.inferred_tags
   339→                if not body.description:
   340→                    inferred_description = analysis.suggested_description
   341→            except Exception as e:
   342→                print(f"[{block_id}] Code analysis failed, using provided values: {e}")
   343→
   344→        # Create full code block
   345→        code_block = CodeBlock(
   346→            id=block_id,
   347→            name=body.name,
   348→            description=inferred_description,
   349→            triggering_query=body.triggering_query,  # Original user query for embedding
   350→            source=source_to_store,
   351→            entrypoint=body.entrypoint,
   352→            input_schema=body.input_schema,
   353→            output_schema=body.output_schema,
   354→            language=body.language,
   355→            language_version=body.language_version,
   356→            dependencies=body.dependencies,
   357→            tags=body.tags if body.tags else inferred_tags,
   358→            capabilities=body.capabilities if body.capabilities else inferred_capabilities,
   359→            example_queries=body.example_queries,
   360→            created_at=now,
   361→            updated_at=now,
   362→        )
   363→
   364→        # Generate sparse vector for hybrid search (keyword/BM25) — primary vector only
   365→        code_search_text = create_code_search_text(
   366→            source=code_block.source,
   367→            name=code_block.name,
   368→            description=code_block.description,
   369→            entrypoint=code_block.entrypoint,
   370→            tags=code_block.tags,
   371→            capabilities=code_block.capabilities,
   372→        )
   373→        sparse_values = encode_sparse(code_search_text)
   374→
   375→        # Create metadata for Pinecone (shared across all facet vectors)
   376→        metadata = {
   377→            "type": "code_block",
   378→            "code_block_id": block_id,
   379→            "name": code_block.name,
   380→            "description": code_block.description,
   381→            "triggering_query": code_block.triggering_query,  # Original user query
   382→            "source": code_block.source,
   383→            "entrypoint": code_block.entrypoint,
   384→            "input_schema": json.dumps(code_block.input_schema),
   385→            "output_schema": json.dumps(code_block.output_schema),
   386→            "language": code_block.language,
   387→            "language_version": code_block.language_version,
   388→            "dependencies": json.dumps(code_block.dependencies),
   389→            "tags": json.dumps(code_block.tags),
   390→            "capabilities": json.dumps(code_block.capabilities),
   391→            "created_at": now.isoformat(),
   392→            "updated_at": now.isoformat(),
   393→            # User tracking for future migration to dedicated namespaces
   394→            "user_id": user_id,
   395→            "stored_at": now.isoformat(),
   396→        }
   397→
   398→        # Run query guess generation and synthetic query generation in parallel
   399→        # (both depend on code_block/metadata from analyze_code, but not on each other)
   400→        async def _generate_facets() -> dict[str, str]:
   401→            """Build facet texts: use basic text builders for all facets, then override query_guesses with Claude-generated guesses."""
   402→            facets = create_all_facet_texts(code_block)
   403→            try:
   404→                guesses = await generate_query_guesses(
   405→                    user_query=code_block.triggering_query or code_block.description or code_block.name,
   406→                    code=code_block.source[:3000],
   407→                )
   408→                facets["query_guesses"] = "\n".join(guesses)
   409→            except Exception as e:
   410→                print(f"[{block_id}] Claude query guess generation failed, using basic text builder: {e}")
   411→            return facets
   412→
   413→        async def _generate_synthetics() -> None:
   414→            """Generate synthetic query variants for this code block."""
   415→            try:
   416→                await process_code_block_synthetics(
   417→                    code_block_id=block_id,
   418→                    metadata=metadata,
   419→                    namespace=namespace,
   420→                )
   421→                print(f"[{block_id}] Synthetic queries generated")
   422→            except Exception as e:
   423→                print(f"[{block_id}] Synthetic query generation failed (non-fatal): {e}")
   424→
   425→        facet_texts, _ = await asyncio.gather(_generate_facets(), _generate_synthetics())
   426→
   427→        # Generate dense embeddings for all facets in a single batch call
   428→        facet_names = list(facet_texts.keys())
   429→        facet_text_list = [facet_texts[f] for f in facet_names]
   430→        facet_embeddings_list = await embed_batch(facet_text_list)
   431→        facet_embeddings = dict(zip(facet_names, facet_embeddings_list))
   432→
   433→        # Upsert all facet vectors to Pinecone (sparse only on primary)
   434→        await upsert_code_block_multi_vector(block_id, facet_embeddings, metadata, sparse_values, namespace=namespace)
   435→
   436→        # Infer origin from tags
   437→        origin = "playground" if body.tags and "playground" in body.tags else "sdk"
   438→
   439→        # Mirror to Postgres for fast dashboard listing
   440→        try:
   441→            log_text = body.execution_logs or None
   442→            upsert_snippet(
   443→                snippet_id=block_id,
   444→                name=code_block.name,
   445→                description=code_block.description or "",
   446→                source=code_block.source,
   447→                language=code_block.language or "python",
   448→                entrypoint=code_block.entrypoint or "main",
   449→                namespace=namespace or "code_blocks",
   450→                triggering_query=code_block.triggering_query,
   451→                user_id=UUID(user_id) if user_id else None,
   452→                organization_id=UUID(organization_id) if organization_id else None,
   453→                origin=origin,
   454→                log_text=log_text,
   455→            )
   456→            register_snippet_source_hash(block_id, code_block.source, namespace or "code_blocks")
   457→        except psycopg2.errors.UniqueViolation:
   458→            print(f"[{block_id}] Duplicate source_hash detected by DB constraint, skipping Postgres mirror")
   459→        except Exception as e:
   460→            print(f"[{block_id}] Postgres snippet mirror failed (non-fatal): {e}")
   461→
   462→        # Invalidate verbatim cache for this namespace, then warm it for the triggering query
   463→        if namespace:
   464→            await invalidate_verbatim_cache_async(namespace)
   465→            if body.triggering_query:
   466→                await warm_verbatim_cache_for_query(body.triggering_query, namespace)
   467→
   468→        # Increment embedding count for free tier tracking
   469→        if user_id:
   470→            try:
   471→                await increment_user_embedding_count_async(UUID(user_id))
   472→            except Exception as e:
   473→                print(f"[{block_id}] Failed to increment embedding count (non-fatal): {e}")
   474→
   475→        # Record metrics
   476→        record_code_block_stored(code_block.language or "unknown")
   477→        print(f"[{block_id}] Code block stored successfully in namespace: {namespace or 'default'}")
   478→
   479→        mark_completed(block_id)
   480→
   481→    except Exception as e:
   482→        print(f"[{block_id}] Background storage failed: {e}")
   483→        mark_failed(block_id, str(e), type(e).__name__)

   491→@router.delete("/code-block/{code_block_id}", response_model=DeleteCodeBlockResponse)
   492→@limiter.limit(STORE_RATE_LIMIT)
   493→async def delete_code_block(
   494→    code_block_id: str,
   495→    request: Request,
   496→    user_info: dict = Depends(enforce_quota),
   497→) -> DeleteCodeBlockResponse:
   498→    """Delete a code block by ID.
   499→
   500→    Admins can delete any code block. Org users can delete code blocks
   501→    stored in their own namespace.
   502→    """
   503→    is_admin = user_info.get("is_admin", False)
   504→    namespace = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "code_blocks")
   505→
   506→    # Verify the code block exists
   507→    block = await fetch_code_block(code_block_id, namespace=namespace)
   508→    if not block and not is_admin:
   509→        raise CodeBlockNotFoundError(code_block_id, namespace=namespace)
   510→
   511→    # Admins can delete from any namespace — try the provided namespace first,
   512→    # then fall back to None (default namespace) if not found
   513→    if is_admin and not block:
   514→        block = await fetch_code_block(code_block_id)
   515→        if not block:
   516→            raise CodeBlockNotFoundError(code_block_id)
   517→        namespace = None  # delete from default namespace
   518→
   519→    await delete_code_block_with_facets(code_block_id, namespace=namespace)
   520→
   521→    # Remove from Postgres mirror
   522→    try:
   523→        delete_snippet_pg(code_block_id)
   524→    except Exception as e:
   525→        print(f"[{code_block_id}] Postgres snippet delete failed (non-fatal): {e}")
   526→
   527→    # Invalidate verbatim cache for this namespace to avoid stale results
   528→    if namespace:
   529→        await invalidate_verbatim_cache_async(namespace)
   530→
   531→    return DeleteCodeBlockResponse(
   532→        success=True,
   533→        code_block_id=code_block_id,
   534→        message=f"Code block {code_block_id} deleted",
   535→    )

   558→@router.post("/execution", response_model=StoreExecutionResponse)
   559→@limiter.limit(STORE_RATE_LIMIT)
   560→async def store_execution(
   561→    request: Request,
   562→    body: StoreExecutionRequest,
   563→    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
   564→    user_info: dict = Depends(enforce_quota),
   565→) -> StoreExecutionResponse:
   566→    """Store an execution record - returns immediately, processing happens in background"""
   567→    # Generate ID immediately so we can return it
   568→    execution_id = generate_id("exec")
   569→
   570→    # Extract user_id for metadata tracking
   571→    user_id = user_info.get("user_id")
   572→
   573→    # Resolve namespaces for all types
   574→    exec_namespace = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "executions")
   575→    task_pattern_namespace = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "task_patterns")
   576→    golden_examples_namespace = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "golden_examples")
   577→
   578→    # Queue all heavy processing for background execution
   579→    background_tasks.add_task(
   580→        _process_execution_storage,
   581→        execution_id=execution_id,
   582→        body=body,
   583→        user_id=user_id,
   584→        exec_namespace=exec_namespace,
   585→        task_pattern_namespace=task_pattern_namespace,
   586→        golden_examples_namespace=golden_examples_namespace,
   587→    )
   588→
   589→    return StoreExecutionResponse(
   590→        success=True,
   591→        execution_id=execution_id,
   592→        pattern_updated=False,  # Will be updated in background
   593→        vote_pending=bool(body.code_block_name and body.code_block_description),
   594→        message="Execution storage queued",
   595→        status_url=f"/api/store/status/{execution_id}",
   596→    )

   598→
   599→async def _process_execution_storage(
   600→    execution_id: str,
   601→    body: StoreExecutionRequest,
   602→    user_id: str | None = None,
   603→    exec_namespace: str | None = None,
   604→    task_pattern_namespace: str | None = None,
   605→    golden_examples_namespace: str | None = None,
   606→) -> None:
   607→    """Background task to process and store an execution record"""
   608→    register_task(execution_id, "execution_storage")
   609→    try:
   610→        now = datetime.utcnow()
   611→
   612→        # Create execution metadata
   613→        metadata = {
   614→            "type": "execution",
   615→            "execution_id": execution_id,
   616→            "code_block_id": body.code_block_id,
   617→            "timestamp": now.isoformat(),
   618→            "execution_state": body.execution_state.value,
   619→            "duration_ms": body.duration_ms,
   620→            "error_message": body.error_message,
   621→            "error_type": body.error_type,
   622→            "triggering_task": body.triggering_task,
   623→            "retrieval_score": 0,
   624→            "verdict": body.verdict.value if body.verdict else AgentVerdict.PENDING.value,
   625→            "io_input_hash": hash_dict(body.io.input_data),
   626→            "io_output_hash": hash_obj(body.io.output_data),
   627→            "io_output_type": body.io.output_type,
   628→            "review": json.dumps(body.review.model_dump()) if body.review else None,
   629→            # User tracking for future migration
   630→            "user_id": user_id,
   631→            "stored_at": now.isoformat(),
   632→        }
   633→
   634→        # Create embedding text
   635→        embedding_text = f"""Task: {body.triggering_task}
   636→Execution state: {body.execution_state.value}
   637→Verdict: {metadata["verdict"]}
   638→Input: {json.dumps(body.io.input_data)[:500]}"""
   639→
   640→        embedding = await embed(embedding_text)
   641→
   642→        # Upsert execution
   643→        await upsert_execution(execution_id, embedding, metadata, namespace=exec_namespace)
   644→
   645→        # Track execution count in Postgres snippet metrics
   646→        try:
   647→            await record_snippet_execution_async(body.code_block_id)
   648→        except Exception as e:
   649→            print(f"[{execution_id}] Postgres execution count update failed (non-fatal): {e}")
   650→
   651→        # Update task pattern based on verdict (if provided)
   652→        if body.verdict and body.verdict != AgentVerdict.PENDING:
   653→            record_verdict(body.verdict.value)
   654→            await update_task_pattern(
   655→                task_pattern=body.triggering_task,
   656→                code_block_id=body.code_block_id,
   657→                verdict=body.verdict,
   658→                execution_state=body.execution_state,
   659→                reasoning=body.review.reasoning if body.review else None,
   660→                namespace=task_pattern_namespace,
   661→            )
   662→
   663→            # If thumbs up and output was correct, store as golden example
   664→            if body.verdict == AgentVerdict.THUMBS_UP and body.review and body.review.output_was_correct:
   665→                await store_golden_example(execution_id, body, namespace=golden_examples_namespace)
   666→
   667→        # Run AI voting if we have code block info
   668→        if body.code_block_name and body.code_block_description:
   669→            await vote_on_execution(
   670→                execution_id=execution_id,
   671→                triggering_task=body.triggering_task,
   672→                execution_state=body.execution_state,
   673→                input_data=body.io.input_data,
   674→                output_data=body.io.output_data,
   675→                code_block_id=body.code_block_id,
   676→                code_block_name=body.code_block_name,
   677→                code_block_description=body.code_block_description,
   678→                error_message=body.error_message,
   679→                exec_namespace=exec_namespace,
   680→                task_pattern_namespace=task_pattern_namespace,
   681→                golden_examples_namespace=golden_examples_namespace,
   682→            )
   683→
   684→        print(f"[{execution_id}] Execution stored successfully")
   685→        mark_completed(execution_id)
   686→
   687→    except Exception as e:
   688→        print(f"[{execution_id}] Background execution storage failed: {e}")
   689→        mark_failed(execution_id, str(e), type(e).__name__)
   690→

   727→
   728→async def update_task_pattern(
   729→    task_pattern: str,
   730→    code_block_id: str,
   731→    verdict: AgentVerdict,
   732→    execution_state: ExecutionState,
   733→    reasoning: str | None = None,
   734→    namespace: str | None = None,
   735→) -> bool:
   736→    """Update task pattern based on execution verdict"""
   737→    pattern_id = f"{hash_str(task_pattern)}_{code_block_id}"
   738→
   739→    # Try to fetch existing pattern
   740→    existing_meta: dict | None = None
   741→    try:
   742→        existing = await fetch_task_pattern(pattern_id, namespace=namespace)
   743→        if existing and existing.metadata:
   744→            existing_meta = dict(existing.metadata)
   745→    except Exception:
   746→        pass
   747→
   748→    now = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
   749→    state_key = execution_state.value.lower()
   750→
   751→    if existing_meta:
   752→        # Update existing pattern
   753→        metadata = existing_meta.copy()
   754→
   755→        if verdict == AgentVerdict.THUMBS_UP:
   756→            metadata["thumbs_up"] = metadata.get("thumbs_up", 0) + 1
   757→            metadata["last_thumbs_up"] = now
   758→            key = f"thumbs_up_when_{state_key}"
   759→            metadata[key] = metadata.get(key, 0) + 1
   760→        elif verdict == AgentVerdict.THUMBS_DOWN:
   761→            metadata["thumbs_down"] = metadata.get("thumbs_down", 0) + 1
   762→            metadata["last_thumbs_down"] = now
   763→            key = f"thumbs_down_when_{state_key}"
   764→            metadata[key] = metadata.get(key, 0) + 1
   765→
   766→            # Track reasons
   767→            if reasoning:
   768→                reasons = json.loads(metadata.get("thumbs_down_reasons", "[]"))
   769→                reasons.append(
   770→                    {
   771→                        "timestamp": now,
   772→                        "reasoning": reasoning,
   773→                        "execution_state": execution_state.value,
   774→                    }
   775→                )
   776→                metadata["thumbs_down_reasons"] = json.dumps(reasons[-10:])  # Keep last 10
   777→    else:
   778→        # Fetch code block to get source for discoverable name generation
   779→        discoverable_name = ""
   780→        try:
   781→            code_block = await fetch_code_block(code_block_id)
   782→            if code_block and code_block.metadata:
   783→                source = code_block.metadata.get("source", "")
   784→                description = code_block.metadata.get("description", "")
   785→                if source:
   786→                    discoverable_name = await generate_discoverable_name(
   787→                        source=source,
   788→                        task=task_pattern,
   789→                        description=description,
   790→                    )
   791→        except Exception as e:
   792→            print(f"Failed to generate discoverable name: {e}")
   793→
   794→        # Create new pattern
   795→        metadata = {
   796→            "type": "task_pattern",
   797→            "task_pattern": task_pattern,
   798→            "code_block_id": code_block_id,
   799→            "code_block_name": discoverable_name,
   800→            "thumbs_up": 1 if verdict == AgentVerdict.THUMBS_UP else 0,
   801→            "thumbs_down": 1 if verdict == AgentVerdict.THUMBS_DOWN else 0,
   802→        }
   803→
   804→        if verdict == AgentVerdict.THUMBS_UP:
   805→            metadata["last_thumbs_up"] = now
   806→            metadata[f"thumbs_up_when_{state_key}"] = 1
   807→        elif verdict == AgentVerdict.THUMBS_DOWN:
   808→            metadata["last_thumbs_down"] = now
   809→            metadata[f"thumbs_down_when_{state_key}"] = 1
   810→            if reasoning:
   811→                metadata["thumbs_down_reasons"] = json.dumps(
   812→                    [{"timestamp": now, "reasoning": reasoning, "execution_state": execution_state.value}]
   813→                )
   814→
   815→    # Generate embedding and sparse vector for task pattern
   816→    embedding = await embed(task_pattern)
   817→    sparse_values = encode_sparse(task_pattern)
   818→
   819→    # Upsert pattern with hybrid search support
   820→    await upsert_task_pattern(pattern_id, embedding, metadata, namespace=namespace, sparse_values=sparse_values)
   821→
   822→    # Invalidate verbatim cache for this namespace
   823→    if namespace:
   824→        await invalidate_verbatim_cache_async(namespace)
   825→
   826→    return True

   829→async def store_golden_example(
   830→    execution_id: str,
   831→    data: StoreExecutionRequest,
   832→    namespace: str | None = None,
   833→) -> None:
   834→    """Store a golden example for few-shot learning"""
   835→    example_id = f"example_{execution_id}"
   836→
   837→    # Fetch code block to get source snippet
   838→    code_snippet = ""
   839→    try:
   840→        code_block = await fetch_code_block(data.code_block_id)
   841→        if code_block and code_block.metadata:
   842→            source = code_block.metadata.get("source", "")
   843→            # Truncate to reasonable size for few-shot learning
   844→            code_snippet = source[:2000] if source else ""
   845→    except Exception as e:
   846→        print(f"Failed to fetch code block for golden example: {e}")
   847→
   848→    metadata = {
   849→        "type": "golden_example",
   850→        "execution_id": execution_id,
   851→        "code_block_id": data.code_block_id,
   852→        "task": data.triggering_task,
   853→        "input_sample": truncate_for_storage(data.io.input_data),
   854→        "output_sample": truncate_for_storage(data.io.output_data),
   855→        "code_snippet": code_snippet,
   856→    }
   857→
   858→    embedding = await embed(data.triggering_task)
   859→    await upsert_golden_example(example_id, embedding, metadata, namespace=namespace)

   928→def detect_language(file_path: str) -> str | None:
   929→    """Detect language from file extension"""
   930→    import os
   931→
   932→    ext = os.path.splitext(file_path)[1].lower()
   933→    return CODE_EXTENSIONS.get(ext)
   934→
   935→
   936→def detect_entrypoint(source: str, language: str) -> str:
   937→    """Detect the main entrypoint function/class in the source code"""
   938→    lines = source.split("\n")
   939→
   940→    patterns = {
   941→        "python": ["def main(", "async def main(", "if __name__"],
   942→        "typescript": ["export default", "export function", "async function main"],
   943→        "javascript": ["module.exports", "export default", "function main"],
   944→        "go": ["func main(", "func Main("],
   945→        "rust": ["fn main(", "pub fn main("],
   946→    }
   947→
   948→    lang_patterns = patterns.get(language, [])
   949→    for line in lines:
   950→        stripped = line.strip()
   951→        for pattern in lang_patterns:
   952→            if pattern in stripped:
   953→                if "def " in stripped or "func " in stripped or "fn " in stripped:
   954→                    parts = stripped.split("(")[0].split()
   955→                    if len(parts) >= 2:
   956→                        return parts[-1]
   957→                elif "function " in stripped:
   958→                    parts = stripped.split("(")[0].replace("function", "").strip()
   959→                    return parts.split()[-1] if parts else "main"
   960→                return "main"
   961→
   962→    # Default: find first function
   963→    for line in lines:
   964→        if language == "python" and line.strip().startswith("def "):
   965→            func_name = line.split("(")[0].replace("def ", "").strip()
   966→            return func_name
   967→        elif language in ("typescript", "javascript") and "function " in line:
   968→            parts = line.split("(")[0]
   969→            if "function " in parts:
   970→                return parts.split("function ")[-1].strip()
   971→
   972→    return "main"
   973→
   974→
   975→def extract_tags(source: str, language: str) -> list[str]:
   976→    """Extract relevant tags from the source code"""
   977→    tags = [language]
   978→    source_lower = source.lower()
   979→
   980→    tag_patterns = {
   981→        "requests": ["import requests", "from requests"],
   982→        "api": ["api", "endpoint", "fetch", "http"],
   983→        "database": ["sqlite", "postgres", "mysql", "mongodb", "database"],
   984→        "async": ["async def", "await ", "asyncio"],
   985→        "web": ["flask", "django", "fastapi", "express", "http.server"],
   986→        "file": ["open(", "read(", "write(", "path"],
   987→        "json": ["json.load", "json.dump", "JSON.parse", "JSON.stringify"],
   988→        "csv": ["csv.reader", "csv.writer", "pandas"],
   989→        "math": ["numpy", "scipy", "math.", "calculate"],
   990→        "ml": ["tensorflow", "pytorch", "sklearn", "machine learning"],
   991→        "test": ["unittest", "pytest", "test_", "assert"],
   992→    }
   993→
   994→    for tag, patterns in tag_patterns.items():
   995→        for pattern in patterns:
   996→            if pattern in source_lower:
   997→                tags.append(tag)
   998→                break
   999→
  1000→    return list(set(tags))
  1001→
  1002→
  1003→def _sanitize_relative_path(path: str) -> Path:
  1004→    """Normalize paths to a safe, relative form."""
  1005→    clean = path.replace("\\", "/")
  1006→    parts = [p for p in clean.split("/") if p and p not in (".", "..")]
  1007→    if not parts:
  1008→        return Path("file")
  1009→    return Path(*parts)
  1010→
  1011→
  1012→def _decode_log_bytes(log_file: LogFile) -> bytes:
  1013→    """Decode a log file payload based on encoding."""
  1014→    if log_file.encoding == "base64":
  1015→        return base64.b64decode(log_file.content)
  1016→    return log_file.content.encode("utf-8", errors="replace")
  1017→
  1018→
  1019→def _derive_task_summary(prompts: list[str]) -> str:
  1020→    """Pick a reasonable task summary from a prompt history."""
  1021→    for prompt in reversed(prompts):
  1022→        if prompt and prompt.strip():
  1023→            return prompt.strip()
  1024→    return "Bulk upload"
  1025→

  1027→async def _store_code_block_from_file(
  1028→    file: FileWritten,
  1029→    task_summary: str,
  1030→    *,
  1031→    user_id: str | None,
  1032→    organization_id: str | None = None,
  1033→    namespace: str | None,
  1034→    run_url: str | None = None,
  1035→    dependencies: dict[str, str] | None = None,
  1036→    source_url: str | None = None,
  1037→    execution_logs: str | None = None,
  1038→) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
  1039→    """Store a single code file and return metadata for voting."""
  1040→    import os
  1041→
  1042→    language = detect_language(file.path)
  1043→    if not language:
  1044→        return None  # Skip non-code files
  1045→
  1046→    if len(file.content) < MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH:
  1047→        print(
  1048→            f"[execution-result] Skipped {file.path}: source too short ({len(file.content)} chars < {MIN_SOURCE_LENGTH})"
  1049→        )
  1050→        return None
  1051→
  1052→    filename = os.path.basename(file.path) if file.path else None
  1053→    ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1] if filename else None
  1054→
  1055→    # Generate descriptive name using AI (extension passed if available)
  1056→    try:
  1057→        name = await generate_discoverable_name(
  1058→            source=file.content,
  1059→            task=task_summary,
  1060→            description=None,
  1061→            extension=ext if ext else None,
  1062→        )
  1063→        print(f"[execution-result] Generated name for {filename or 'inline code'}: {name}")
  1064→    except Exception as e:
  1065→        # Fallback to filename if AI naming fails
  1066→        name = filename or "unnamed_snippet.py"
  1067→        print(f"[execution-result] AI naming failed for {filename or 'inline code'}, using fallback: {e}")
  1068→
  1069→    # Use fast local detection (no Claude API calls)
  1070→    entrypoint = detect_entrypoint(file.content, language)
  1071→    tags = extract_tags(file.content, language)
  1072→
  1073→    # Generate ID and timestamp
  1074→    block_id = generate_id("cb")
  1075→    now = datetime.utcnow()
  1076→
  1077→    # Generate AI description via analyze_code
  1078→    description = f"Generated for task: {task_summary[:100]}"
  1079→    try:
  1080→        analysis = await analyze_code(file.content, entrypoint)
  1081→        description = analysis.suggested_description
  1082→        tags = tags or analysis.inferred_tags
  1083→        print(f"[execution-result] Generated description: {description}")
  1084→    except Exception as e:
  1085→        print(f"[execution-result] analyze_code failed, using fallback: {e}")
  1086→
  1087→    code_block = CodeBlock(
  1088→        id=block_id,
  1089→        name=name,
  1090→        description=description,
  1091→        triggering_query=task_summary,  # Original user query for embedding
  1092→        source=file.content,
  1093→        entrypoint=entrypoint,
  1094→        input_schema={},
  1095→        output_schema={},
  1096→        language=language,
  1097→        language_version=None,
  1098→        dependencies=dependencies or {},
  1099→        tags=tags,
  1100→        capabilities=[],
  1101→        example_queries=[task_summary],
  1102→        created_at=now,
  1103→        updated_at=now,
  1104→    )
  1105→
  1106→    # Generate multi-facet embeddings (no Claude call for speed)
  1107→    facet_texts = create_all_facet_texts(code_block)
  1108→    facet_names = list(facet_texts.keys())
  1109→    facet_text_list = [facet_texts[f] for f in facet_names]
  1110→    facet_embeddings_list = await embed_batch(facet_text_list)
  1111→    facet_embeddings = dict(zip(facet_names, facet_embeddings_list))
  1112→
  1113→    code_search_text = create_code_search_text(
  1114→        source=code_block.source,
  1115→        name=code_block.name,
  1116→        description=code_block.description,
  1117→        entrypoint=code_block.entrypoint,
  1118→        tags=code_block.tags,
  1119→        capabilities=code_block.capabilities,
  1120→    )
  1121→    sparse_values = encode_sparse(code_search_text)
  1122→
  1123→    metadata = {
  1124→        "type": "code_block",
  1125→        "code_block_id": block_id,
  1126→        "name": code_block.name,
  1127→        "description": code_block.description,
  1128→        "triggering_query": code_block.triggering_query,  # Original user query
  1129→        "source": code_block.source,
  1130→        "entrypoint": code_block.entrypoint,
  1131→        "input_schema": json.dumps(code_block.input_schema),
  1132→        "output_schema": json.dumps(code_block.output_schema),
  1133→        "language": code_block.language,
  1134→        "language_version": code_block.language_version,
  1135→        "dependencies": json.dumps(code_block.dependencies),
  1136→        "tags": json.dumps(code_block.tags),
  1137→        "capabilities": json.dumps(code_block.capabilities),
  1138→        "created_at": now.isoformat(),
  1139→        "updated_at": now.isoformat(),
  1140→        # User tracking for future migration
  1141→        "user_id": user_id,
  1142→        "stored_at": now.isoformat(),
  1143→        "source_url": source_url,
  1144→    }
  1145→
  1146→    await upsert_code_block_multi_vector(block_id, facet_embeddings, metadata, sparse_values, namespace=namespace)
  1147→
  1148→    # Mirror to Postgres for fast dashboard listing
  1149→    try:
  1150→        log_text = execution_logs or None
  1151→        upsert_snippet(
  1152→            snippet_id=block_id,
  1153→            name=name,
  1154→            description=description,
  1155→            source=file.content,
  1156→            language=language,
  1157→            entrypoint=entrypoint,
  1158→            namespace=namespace or "code_blocks",
  1159→            triggering_query=task_summary,
  1160→            user_id=UUID(user_id) if user_id else None,
  1161→            organization_id=UUID(organization_id) if organization_id else None,
  1162→            run_url=run_url,
  1163→            source_url=source_url,
  1164→            filename=filename,
  1165→            log_text=log_text,
  1166→        )
  1167→        register_snippet_source_hash(block_id, file.content, namespace or "code_blocks")
  1168→    except psycopg2.errors.UniqueViolation:
  1169→        print(f"[{block_id}] Duplicate source_hash detected by DB constraint, skipping Postgres mirror")
  1170→    except Exception as e:
  1171→        print(f"[{block_id}] Postgres snippet mirror failed (non-fatal): {e}")
  1172→
  1173→    # Invalidate verbatim cache for this namespace, then warm it for the task
  1174→    if namespace:
  1175→        await invalidate_verbatim_cache_async(namespace)
  1176→        if task_summary:
  1177→            await warm_verbatim_cache_for_query(task_summary, namespace)
  1178→
  1179→    # Increment embedding count for free tier tracking
  1180→    if user_id:
  1181→        try:
  1182→            await increment_user_embedding_count_async(UUID(user_id))
  1183→        except Exception as e:
  1184→            print(f"[{block_id}] Failed to increment embedding count (non-fatal): {e}")
  1185→
  1186→    print(f"[{block_id}] Code block stored: {name} (namespace: {namespace or 'default'})")
  1187→
  1188→    return {
  1189→        "block_id": block_id,
  1190→        "name": name,
  1191→        "description": description,
  1192→        "filename": filename,
  1193→        "language": language,
  1194→        "source_preview": file.content[:500] if file.content else "",
  1195→    }

  1198→@router.post("/execution-result", response_model=SubmitExecutionResultResponse)
  1199→@limiter.limit(STORE_RATE_LIMIT)
  1200→async def submit_execution_result(
  1201→    request: Request,
  1202→    body: SubmitExecutionResultRequest,
  1203→    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
  1204→    user_info: dict = Depends(enforce_quota),
  1205→) -> SubmitExecutionResultResponse:
  1206→    """
  1207→    Submit raw execution result from SDK - returns immediately, processing in background.
  1208→
  1209→    Flow:
  1210→    1. Returns immediately with queued status
  1211→    2. Background task filters files to only store code files
  1212→    3. Background task processes embeddings and upserts to Pinecone
  1213→    """
  1214→    if not body.succeeded:
  1215→        return SubmitExecutionResultResponse(
  1216→            success=True,
  1217→            code_blocks_stored=0,
  1218→            message="Skipped storage: task did not succeed",
  1219→        )
  1220→
  1221→    # Check if the single file is a code file
  1222→    is_code_file = bool(detect_language(body.file_written.path))
  1223→
  1224→    # Extract user_id and organization_id for metadata tracking
  1225→    user_id = user_info.get("user_id")
  1226→    organization_id = user_info.get("organization_id")
  1227→
  1228→    # Check embedding limit for free tier users before queuing storage
  1229→    tier_str = user_info.get("organization_tier") or user_info.get("tier", "free")
  1230→    if user_id and is_code_file and tier_str in ("free", "dev", None):
  1231→        allowed, current, limit = await check_embedding_limit_async(UUID(user_id), tier_str or "free")
  1232→        if not allowed:
  1233→            raise EmbeddingLimitReachedError(current=current, limit=limit)
  1234→
  1235→    # Get namespace for storage (respects X-Raysurfer-Namespace header)
  1236→    namespace = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "code_blocks", public=body.public)
  1237→
  1238→    # Enforce public upload quota/billing
  1239→    if body.public:
  1240→        tier = _get_tier(user_info)
  1241→        if tier == "free" and user_id:
  1242→            await enforce_public_quota(user_id, "public_upload", tier)
  1243→        if tier == "pro":
  1244→            from app.lib.credit_service import get_credit_balance
  1245→
  1246→            org_id = user_info.get("organization_id")
  1247→            if org_id:
  1248→                balance = await get_credit_balance(UUID(org_id))
  1249→                if balance < 5:
  1250→                    from app.errors import InsufficientCreditError
  1251→
  1252→                    raise InsufficientCreditError(balance_cents=balance, required_cents=5, org_id=org_id)
  1253→            request.state.credit_operation = "public_upload"
  1254→
  1255→    # Generate a task_id for status tracking
  1256→    result_task_id = generate_id("ert")
  1257→
  1258→    # Resolve all namespaces needed for auto-voting
  1259→    exec_ns_for_auto = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "executions")
  1260→    tp_ns_for_auto = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "task_patterns")
  1261→    ge_ns_for_auto = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "golden_examples")
  1262→
  1263→    # Queue code file storage if it's a code file
  1264→    if is_code_file:
  1265→        background_tasks.add_task(
  1266→            _process_execution_result_storage,
  1267→            body=body,
  1268→            task_id=result_task_id,
  1269→            user_id=user_id,
  1270→            organization_id=organization_id,
  1271→            namespace=namespace,
  1272→            auto_vote=body.effective_ai_voting,
  1273→            user_vote=body.user_vote,
  1274→            execution_logs=body.execution_logs,
  1275→            exec_namespace=exec_ns_for_auto,
  1276→            task_pattern_namespace=tp_ns_for_auto,
  1277→            golden_examples_namespace=ge_ns_for_auto,
  1278→            run_url=body.run_url,
  1279→        )
  1280→
  1281→    # Resolve namespaces for voting pipeline
  1282→    exec_ns = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "executions")
  1283→    tp_ns = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "task_patterns")
  1284→    ge_ns = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "golden_examples")
  1285→
  1286→    # If cached code blocks were used and task succeeded, trigger voting
  1287→    # (do this even if no new code file to store)
  1288→    if body.succeeded and body.cached_code_blocks:
  1289→        print(f"[voting] Triggering voting for {len(body.cached_code_blocks)} cached blocks")
  1290→        for cached_block in body.cached_code_blocks:
  1291→            # Track execution count in Postgres snippet metrics
  1292→            try:
  1293→                await record_snippet_execution_async(cached_block.code_block_id)
  1294→            except Exception as e:
  1295→                print(
  1296→                    f"[voting] Postgres execution count update failed for {cached_block.code_block_id} (non-fatal): {e}"
  1297→                )
  1298→
  1299→            print(f"[voting] Queueing vote for: {cached_block.code_block_id} ({cached_block.filename})")
  1300→            background_tasks.add_task(
  1301→                vote_on_execution,
  1302→                execution_id=f"cache_usage_{generate_id('cu')}",
  1303→                triggering_task=body.task,
  1304→                execution_state=ExecutionState.COMPLETED,
  1305→                input_data={"task": body.task},
  1306→                output_data={"succeeded": True},
  1307→                code_block_id=cached_block.code_block_id,
  1308→                code_block_name=cached_block.filename,
  1309→                code_block_description=cached_block.description,
  1310→                error_message=None,
  1311→                user_id=user_id,
  1312→                organization_id=organization_id,
  1313→                exec_namespace=exec_ns,
  1314→                task_pattern_namespace=tp_ns,
  1315→                golden_examples_namespace=ge_ns,
  1316→                code_blocks_namespace=namespace,
  1317→            )
  1318→    else:
  1319→        if not body.succeeded:
  1320→            print("[voting] Skipping voting - task did not succeed")
  1321→        elif not body.cached_code_blocks:
  1322→            print("[voting] Skipping voting - no cached code blocks provided")
  1323→
  1324→    vote_count = len(body.cached_code_blocks) if body.cached_code_blocks else 0
  1325→
  1326→    # Build message based on what was done
  1327→    message_parts = []
  1328→    if is_code_file:
  1329→        message_parts.append("Storage queued for 1 code file")
  1330→    if vote_count > 0:
  1331→        message_parts.append(f"voting queued for {vote_count} cached blocks")
  1332→
  1333→    message = ", ".join(message_parts) if message_parts else "No actions needed"
  1334→
  1335→    return SubmitExecutionResultResponse(
  1336→        success=True,
  1337→        code_blocks_stored=1 if is_code_file else 0,
  1338→        message=message,
  1339→        status_url=f"/api/store/status/{result_task_id}" if is_code_file else None,
  1340→    )

  1343→@router.post("/bulk-execution-result", response_model=BulkExecutionResultResponse)
  1344→@limiter.limit(STORE_RATE_LIMIT)
  1345→async def submit_bulk_execution_result(
  1346→    request: Request,

  1349→    user_info: dict = Depends(enforce_quota),

  1358→        )
  1359→
  1360→    # Count code files to give user an estimate
  1361→    code_files_count = sum(1 for f in body.files_written if detect_language(f.path))
  1362→
  1363→    # Extract user_id and organization_id for metadata tracking
  1364→    user_id = user_info.get("user_id")
  1365→    organization_id = user_info.get("organization_id")
  1366→
  1367→    # Check embedding limit for free tier users before queuing storage
  1368→    tier_str = user_info.get("organization_tier") or user_info.get("tier", "free")
  1369→    if user_id and code_files_count > 0 and tier_str in ("free", "dev", None):
  1370→        allowed, current, limit = await check_embedding_limit_async(UUID(user_id), tier_str or "free")
  1371→        if not allowed:
  1372→            raise EmbeddingLimitReachedError(current=current, limit=limit)
  1373→
  1374→    # Get namespace for storage (respects X-Raysurfer-Namespace header)
  1375→    namespace = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "code_blocks", public=body.public)
  1376→
  1377→    # Enforce public upload quota/billing
  1378→    if body.public:
  1379→        tier = _get_tier(user_info)
  1380→        if tier == "free" and user_id:
  1381→            await enforce_public_quota(user_id, "public_upload", tier)
  1382→        if tier == "pro":
  1383→            from app.lib.credit_service import get_credit_balance
  1384→
  1385→            org_id = user_info.get("organization_id")
  1386→            if org_id:
  1387→                balance = await get_credit_balance(UUID(org_id))
  1388→                if balance < 5:
  1389→                    from app.errors import InsufficientCreditError
  1390→
  1391→                    raise InsufficientCreditError(balance_cents=balance, required_cents=5, org_id=org_id)
  1392→            request.state.credit_operation = "public_upload"
  1393→
  1394→    # Resolve all namespaces needed for voting
  1395→    exec_ns = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "executions")
  1396→    tp_ns = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "task_patterns")
  1397→    ge_ns = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "golden_examples")
  1398→
  1399→    # Generate a task_id for status tracking
  1400→    result_task_id = generate_id("ber")
  1401→
  1402→    background_tasks.add_task(
  1403→        _process_bulk_execution_result,
  1404→        body=body,
  1405→        task_id=result_task_id,
  1406→        user_id=user_id,
  1407→        organization_id=organization_id,
  1408→        namespace=namespace,
  1409→        exec_namespace=exec_ns,
  1410→        task_pattern_namespace=tp_ns,
  1411→        golden_examples_namespace=ge_ns,
  1412→    )
  1413→
  1414→    ai_voting = body.effective_ai_voting
  1415→    has_user_votes = body.user_votes is not None
  1416→
  1417→    message = "Bulk storage queued"
  1418→    if ai_voting and code_files_count > 0:
  1419→        message = f"{message}, grading queued for {code_files_count} code files"
  1420→    elif has_user_votes:
  1421→        message = f"{message}, user votes will be applied for {len(body.user_votes or {})} files"
  1422→
  1423→    votes_queued = code_files_count if ai_voting else (len(body.user_votes or {}) if has_user_votes else 0)
  1424→
  1425→    return BulkExecutionResultResponse(
  1426→        success=True,
  1427→        code_blocks_stored=code_files_count,
  1428→        votes_queued=votes_queued,
  1429→        message=message,
  1430→        status_url=f"/api/store/status/{result_task_id}",
  1431→    )

  1434→async def _process_execution_result_storage(
  1435→    body: SubmitExecutionResultRequest,
  1436→    task_id: str,
  1437→    user_id: str | None = None,
  1438→    organization_id: str | None = None,
  1439→    namespace: str | None = None,
  1440→    auto_vote: bool = True,
  1441→    user_vote: int | None = None,
  1442→    execution_logs: str | None = None,
  1443→    exec_namespace: str | None = None,
  1444→    task_pattern_namespace: str | None = None,
  1445→    golden_examples_namespace: str | None = None,
  1446→    run_url: str | None = None,
  1447→) -> None:
  1448→    """Background task to process and store a single execution result file"""
  1449→
  1450→    register_task(task_id, "execution_result_storage")
  1451→
  1452→    try:
  1453→        stored = await _store_code_block_from_file(
  1454→            body.file_written,
  1455→            body.task,
  1456→            user_id=user_id,
  1457→            organization_id=organization_id,
  1458→            namespace=namespace,
  1459→            run_url=run_url,
  1460→            dependencies=body.dependencies,
  1461→            execution_logs=execution_logs,
  1462→        )
  1463→        if not stored:
  1464→            mark_completed(task_id)
  1465→            print("[execution-result] Skipped: file not a recognized code type")
  1466→            return
  1467→
  1468→        block_id = stored["block_id"]
  1469→        name = stored["name"]
  1470→        description = stored["description"]
  1471→
  1472→        # Apply user-provided vote directly (skips AI voting)
  1473→        if user_vote is not None and body.succeeded:
  1474→            try:
  1475→                verdict = AgentVerdict.THUMBS_UP if user_vote >= 1 else AgentVerdict.THUMBS_DOWN
  1476→                execution_state = ExecutionState.COMPLETED if user_vote >= 1 else ExecutionState.ERRORED
  1477→                effective_vote_source = body.vote_source or "human"
  1478→                await apply_vote_verdict(

  1496→                print(f"[{block_id}] User vote applied: {verdict.value} (count={body.vote_count})")
  1497→            except Exception as e:
  1498→                print(f"[{block_id}] User vote failed (non-fatal): {e}")
  1499→        # Auto-vote on the newly stored block if enabled and task succeeded
  1500→        elif auto_vote and body.succeeded:
  1501→            try:
  1502→                output_data: dict[str, Any] = {
  1503→                    "source_preview": stored.get("source_preview", ""),
  1504→                    "auto_voted": True,
  1505→                }
  1506→                if execution_logs:
  1507→                    output_data["execution_logs"] = truncate_logs_for_ai(execution_logs)
  1508→
  1509→                await _vote_on_execution_async(
  1510→                    execution_id=f"auto_vote_{block_id}",
  1511→                    triggering_task=body.task,
  1512→                    execution_state=ExecutionState.COMPLETED,
  1513→                    input_data={"task": body.task},
  1514→                    output_data=output_data,
  1515→                    code_block_id=block_id,
  1516→                    code_block_name=name,
  1517→                    code_block_description=description,
  1518→                    error_message=None,
  1519→                    user_id=user_id,
  1520→                    exec_namespace=exec_namespace,
  1521→                    task_pattern_namespace=task_pattern_namespace,
  1522→                    golden_examples_namespace=golden_examples_namespace,
  1523→                    code_blocks_namespace=namespace,
  1524→                )
  1525→                print(f"[{block_id}] Auto-voted successfully")
  1526→            except Exception as e:

  1528→
  1529→        mark_completed(task_id)
  1530→        print(f"[execution-result] Stored 1 code block: {name}")
  1531→
  1532→    except Exception as e:
  1533→        mark_failed(task_id, f"Failed to store {body.file_written.path}: {e}", type(e).__name__)
  1534→        print(f"[execution-result] Failed to store {body.file_written.path}: {e}")
  1535→
  1536→
  1537→async def _process_bulk_execution_result(
  1538→    body: BulkExecutionResultRequest,
  1539→    task_id: str,
  1540→    user_id: str | None = None,
  1541→    organization_id: str | None = None,
  1542→    namespace: str | None = None,
  1543→    exec_namespace: str | None = None,
  1544→    task_pattern_namespace: str | None = None,
  1545→    golden_examples_namespace: str | None = None,
  1546→) -> None:
  1547→    """Background task to process a bulk execution upload and apply votes."""
  1548→    register_task(task_id, "bulk_execution_result")
  1549→
  1550→    errors: list[str] = []
  1551→    stored_blocks: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
  1552→
  1553→    task_summary = _derive_task_summary(body.prompts)
  1554→
  1555→    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="raysurfer_bulk_") as tmpdir:
  1556→        base_dir = Path(tmpdir)
  1557→        logs_dir = base_dir / "logs"
  1558→        code_dir = base_dir / "code"
  1559→        logs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  1560→        code_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  1561→
  1562→        # Write prompts
  1563→        try:
  1564→            (base_dir / "prompts.json").write_text(
  1565→                json.dumps(body.prompts, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2),
  1566→                encoding="utf-8",
  1567→            )
  1568→            (base_dir / "prompts.txt").write_text(
  1569→                "\n".join(body.prompts),
  1570→                encoding="utf-8",
  1571→                errors="replace",
  1572→            )
  1573→        except Exception as e:
  1574→            errors.append(f"Failed to write prompts: {e}")
  1575→
  1576→        # Write log files
  1577→        log_paths: list[str] = []
  1578→        if body.log_files:
  1579→            for log_file in body.log_files:
  1580→                try:
  1581→                    rel_path = _sanitize_relative_path(log_file.path)
  1582→                    dest = logs_dir / rel_path
  1583→                    dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  1584→                    dest.write_bytes(_decode_log_bytes(log_file))
  1585→                    log_paths.append(rel_path.as_posix())
  1586→                except Exception as e:
  1587→                    errors.append(f"log {log_file.path}: {e}")
  1588→
  1589→        # Write code files and store code blocks
  1590→        for file in body.files_written:
  1591→            try:
  1592→                rel_path = _sanitize_relative_path(file.path)
  1593→                dest = code_dir / rel_path
  1594→                dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  1595→                dest.write_text(file.content, encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
  1596→
  1597→                stored = await _store_code_block_from_file(
  1598→                    file,
  1599→                    task_summary,
  1600→                    user_id=user_id,
  1601→                    organization_id=organization_id,
  1602→                    namespace=namespace,
  1603→                )
  1604→                if stored:
  1605→                    stored_blocks[rel_path.as_posix()] = stored
  1606→            except Exception as e:
  1607→                errors.append(f"code {file.path}: {e}")
  1608→
  1609→        if not stored_blocks:
  1610→            mark_failed(task_id, "No valid code files stored", "NoCodeFiles")
  1611→            return
  1612→
  1613→        # Write code file manifest for the grader
  1614→        try:
  1615→            code_paths = list(stored_blocks.keys())
  1616→            (base_dir / "code_files.json").write_text(
  1617→                json.dumps(code_paths, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2),
  1618→                encoding="utf-8",
  1619→            )
  1620→            (base_dir / "code_files.txt").write_text(
  1621→                "\n".join(code_paths),
  1622→                encoding="utf-8",
  1623→            )
  1624→        except Exception as e:
  1625→            errors.append(f"Failed to write code file manifest: {e}")
  1626→
  1627→        # Apply user-provided votes if given (skip AI grading)
  1628→        if body.user_votes is not None:
  1629→            effective_vote_source = body.vote_source or "human"
  1630→            for filename, vote_value in body.user_votes.items():
  1631→                normalized = _sanitize_relative_path(filename).as_posix()
  1632→                target = stored_blocks.get(normalized)
  1633→                if not target:
  1634→                    base = Path(normalized).name
  1635→                    for path, block in stored_blocks.items():
  1636→                        if Path(path).name == base:
  1637→                            target = block
  1638→                            break
  1639→                if not target:
  1640→                    errors.append(f"User vote for unknown file: {filename}")
  1641→                    continue
  1642→                verdict = AgentVerdict.THUMBS_UP if vote_value >= 1 else AgentVerdict.THUMBS_DOWN
  1643→                execution_state = ExecutionState.COMPLETED if vote_value >= 1 else ExecutionState.ERRORED
  1644→                try:
  1645→                    await apply_vote_verdict(
  1646→                        execution_id=f"user_vote_{target['block_id']}",
  1647→                        triggering_task=task_summary,
  1648→                        execution_state=execution_state,
  1649→                        input_data={"task": task_summary, "code_file": normalized},
  1650→                        output_data={"user_voted": True, "bulk": True},
  1651→                        code_block_id=target["block_id"],
  1652→                        code_block_name=target["name"],
  1653→                        verdict=verdict,
  1654→                        reasoning="User-provided vote",
  1655→                        user_id=user_id,
  1656→                        exec_namespace=exec_namespace,
  1657→                        task_pattern_namespace=task_pattern_namespace,
  1658→                        golden_examples_namespace=golden_examples_namespace,
  1659→                        code_blocks_namespace=namespace,
  1660→                        vote_source=effective_vote_source,
  1661→                        vote_count=body.vote_count,
  1662→                    )
  1663→                except Exception as e:
  1664→                    errors.append(f"User vote apply failed for {filename}: {e}")
  1665→
  1666→            if errors:
  1667→                mark_failed(task_id, f"Bulk storage completed with errors: {'; '.join(errors[:3])}", "PartialFailure")
  1668→            else:
  1669→                mark_completed(task_id)
  1670→            return
  1671→
  1672→        # Skip grading if disabled
  1673→        if not body.effective_ai_voting:
  1674→            if errors:
  1675→                mark_failed(task_id, f"Bulk storage completed with errors: {'; '.join(errors[:3])}", "PartialFailure")
  1676→            else:
  1677→                mark_completed(task_id)
  1678→            return
  1679→
  1680→        # Run sandboxed bulk grader
  1681→        try:
  1682→            votes = await run_bulk_grader(base_dir)
  1683→        except Exception as e:
  1684→            mark_failed(task_id, f"Bulk grader failed: {e}", type(e).__name__)
  1685→            return
  1686→
  1687→        basename_map: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
  1688→        for path in stored_blocks.keys():
  1689→            base = Path(path).name
  1690→            basename_map.setdefault(base, []).append(path)
  1691→
  1692→        applied_paths: set[str] = set()
  1693→
  1694→        base_input: dict[str, Any] = {
  1695→            "prompt_count": len(body.prompts),
  1696→            "prompts": body.prompts[:10],
  1697→            "log_files": log_paths[:50],
  1698→            "log_file_count": len(log_paths),
  1699→        }
  1700→
  1701→        for vote in votes:
  1702→            raw_path = vote.get("path", "")
  1703→            if raw_path.startswith("code/"):
  1704→                raw_path = raw_path[len("code/") :]
  1705→
  1706→            normalized = _sanitize_relative_path(raw_path).as_posix()
  1707→            target = stored_blocks.get(normalized)
  1708→            if not target:
  1709→                base = Path(normalized).name
  1710→                if base in basename_map and len(basename_map[base]) == 1:
  1711→                    normalized = basename_map[base][0]
  1712→                    target = stored_blocks.get(normalized)
  1713→
  1714→            if not target:
  1715→                errors.append(f"Unknown vote path: {raw_path}")
  1716→                continue
  1717→
  1718→            verdict_raw = str(vote.get("verdict", "")).lower().strip()
  1719→            if verdict_raw in ("thumbs_up", "up", "true", "yes"):
  1720→                verdict = AgentVerdict.THUMBS_UP
  1721→                execution_state = ExecutionState.COMPLETED
  1722→            elif verdict_raw in ("thumbs_down", "down", "false", "no"):
  1723→                verdict = AgentVerdict.THUMBS_DOWN
  1724→                execution_state = ExecutionState.ERRORED
  1725→            else:
  1726→                errors.append(f"Invalid verdict for {raw_path}: {vote.get('verdict')}")
  1727→                continue
  1728→
  1729→            reasoning = str(vote.get("reasoning", ""))[:500]
  1730→
  1731→            input_data = {**base_input, "code_file": normalized}
  1732→            output_data = {"bulk_vote": True, "grader": "claude-agent-sdk"}
  1733→
  1734→            try:
  1735→                bulk_vote_source = body.vote_source or "ai"
  1736→                await apply_vote_verdict(
  1737→                    execution_id=f"bulk_vote_{target['block_id']}",
  1738→                    triggering_task=task_summary,
  1739→                    execution_state=execution_state,
  1740→                    input_data=input_data,
  1741→                    output_data=output_data,
  1742→                    code_block_id=target["block_id"],
  1743→                    code_block_name=target["name"],
  1744→                    verdict=verdict,
  1745→                    reasoning=reasoning,
  1746→                    user_id=user_id,
  1747→                    exec_namespace=exec_namespace,
  1748→                    task_pattern_namespace=task_pattern_namespace,
  1749→                    golden_examples_namespace=golden_examples_namespace,
  1750→                    code_blocks_namespace=namespace,
  1751→                    vote_source=bulk_vote_source,
  1752→                    vote_count=body.vote_count,
  1753→                )
  1754→                applied_paths.add(normalized)
  1755→            except Exception as e:
  1756→                errors.append(f"Vote apply failed for {normalized}: {e}")
  1757→
  1758→        missing = set(stored_blocks.keys()) - applied_paths
  1759→        if missing:
  1760→            errors.append(f"Missing votes for {len(missing)} code files")
  1761→
  1762→        if errors:
  1763→            mark_failed(task_id, f"Bulk grading completed with errors: {'; '.join(errors[:3])}", "PartialFailure")
  1764→        else:
  1765→            mark_completed(task_id)
  1766→
  1767→
  1768→# ============================================================================
  1769→# Record Cache Usage (triggers background voting)
  1770→# ============================================================================
  1771→
  1772→
  1773→@router.post("/cache-usage", response_model=RecordCacheUsageResponse)
  1774→@limiter.limit(STORE_RATE_LIMIT)
  1775→async def record_cache_usage(
  1776→    request: Request,
  1777→    body: RecordCacheUsageRequest,
  1778→    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
  1779→    user_info: dict = Depends(enforce_quota),
  1780→) -> RecordCacheUsageResponse:
  1781→    """
  1782→    Record that a cached code block was used for a task.
  1783→
  1784→    This triggers background voting to assess whether the cached code
  1785→    actually helped complete the task successfully.
  1786→    """
  1787→    try:
  1788→        # Determine execution state based on success
  1789→        execution_state = ExecutionState.COMPLETED if body.succeeded else ExecutionState.ERRORED
  1790→
  1791→        # Extract user_id and organization_id for metadata tracking
  1792→        user_id = user_info.get("user_id")
  1793→        organization_id = user_info.get("organization_id")
  1794→
  1795→        # Resolve namespaces for voting pipeline
  1796→        exec_ns = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "executions")
  1797→        tp_ns = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "task_patterns")
  1798→        ge_ns = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "golden_examples")
  1799→
  1800→        # Resolve code_blocks namespace for thumbs update
  1801→        cb_ns = get_storage_namespace(user_info, "code_blocks")
  1802→
  1803→        # Track execution count in Postgres snippet metrics
  1804→        try:
  1805→            await record_snippet_execution_async(body.code_block_id)
  1806→        except Exception as e:
  1807→            print(f"[cache-usage] Postgres execution count update failed (non-fatal): {e}")
  1808→
  1809→        # Queue background voting
  1810→        background_tasks.add_task(
  1811→            vote_on_execution,
  1812→            execution_id=f"cache_usage_{generate_id('cu')}",
  1813→            triggering_task=body.task,
  1814→            execution_state=execution_state,
  1815→            input_data={"task": body.task},
  1816→            output_data={"succeeded": body.succeeded},
  1817→            code_block_id=body.code_block_id,
  1818→            code_block_name=body.code_block_name,
  1819→            code_block_description=body.code_block_description,
  1820→            error_message=None if body.succeeded else "Task failed",
  1821→            user_id=user_id,
  1822→            organization_id=organization_id,
  1823→            exec_namespace=exec_ns,
  1824→            task_pattern_namespace=tp_ns,
  1825→            golden_examples_namespace=ge_ns,
  1826→            code_blocks_namespace=cb_ns,
  1827→        )
  1828→
  1829→        return RecordCacheUsageResponse(
  1830→            success=True,
  1831→            vote_pending=True,
  1832→            message="Cache usage recorded, voting queued",
  1833→        )
  1834→
  1835→    except Exception as e:
  1836→        raise ExternalServiceError("cache-usage", detail=str(e))
  1837→
  1838→
  1839→# ============================================================================
  1840→# Synthetic Query Generation
  1841→# ============================================================================
  1842→
  1843→
  1844→@router.post("/synthetic-queries", response_model=SyntheticQueriesResponse)
  1845→@limiter.limit(STORE_RATE_LIMIT)
  1846→async def generate_synthetic_queries_endpoint(
  1847→    request: Request,
  1848→    body: SyntheticQueriesRequest,
  1849→    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
  1850→    user_info: dict = Depends(enforce_quota),
  1851→) -> SyntheticQueriesResponse:
  1852→    """
  1853→    Generate synthetic query variants for all code blocks in a namespace.
  1854→
  1855→    Processing happens in the background. Returns immediately.
  1856→    """
  1857→    background_tasks.add_task(
  1858→        _run_synthetic_population,
  1859→        namespace=body.namespace,
  1860→        batch_size=body.batch_size,
  1861→        limit=body.limit,
  1862→        force=body.force,
  1863→        synth_version=body.synth_version,
  1864→    )
  1865→
  1866→    return SyntheticQueriesResponse(
  1867→        success=True,
  1868→        message="Synthetic query generation queued",
  1869→    )
  1870→
  1871→
  1872→def _run_synthetic_population(
  1873→    namespace: str | None,
  1874→    batch_size: int,
  1875→    limit: int | None,
  1876→    force: bool,
  1877→    synth_version: int,
  1878→) -> None:
  1879→    """Sync wrapper to run populate_namespace in a new event loop."""
  1880→    import asyncio
  1881→
  1882→    stats = asyncio.run(
  1883→        populate_namespace(
  1884→            namespace=namespace,
  1885→            batch_size=batch_size,
  1886→            limit=limit,
  1887→            force=force,
  1888→            synth_version=synth_version,
  1889→        )
  1890→    )
  1891→    print(
  1892→        f"[synthetic-queries] Completed: processed={stats.get('processed', 0)}, "
  1893→        f"skipped={stats.get('skipped', 0)}, failed={stats.get('failed', 0)}"
  1894→    )

1→"""
     2→Background Voting Task
